title: Sanger Tree of Life HMW DNA Extraction: Automated Modified Omega Bio-Tek E.Z.N.A.
author: Amy Denton

steps.title: Prepare tubes for the samples being...
steps.instruction: Prepare tubes for the samples being processed depending upon the method of disruption:

steps.title: For samples that require powermashing, label 1
steps.instruction: For samples that require powermashing, label 1.5 mL BioMasher tubes for each sample being processed. Save the BioMasher pestles for later use in their sterile packet, also labelling them for each sample.

steps.title: For samples that have been cryogenically disrupted (e
steps.instruction: For samples that have been cryogenically disrupted (e.g. via bead beating), label 2 mL microcentrifuge tubes for each sample being processed.

steps.title: Transfer the samples into their corresponding 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the samples into their corresponding 1.5 mL BioMasher tube or 2 mL microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Add 350 μL of ML1 buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 350 μL of ML1 buffer and 25 μL Proteinase K solution to all samples.

steps.title: Homogenise samples within the lysis buffer...
steps.instruction: Homogenise samples within the lysis buffer according to sample type and disruption method:

steps.title: For samples which require powermashing, use...
steps.instruction: For samples which require powermashing, use the BioMasher pestle and Diagnocine PowerMasher II tissue disruptor to disrupt the sample within the buffer, following Sanger Tree of Life Sample Homogenisation: PowerMash protocol.

steps.title: For samples which have been cryogenically...
steps.instruction: For samples which have been cryogenically disrupted, pipette-mix samples gently with a wide-bore pipette tip 10 times to homogenise.

steps.title: Incubate samples for at least 30 minutes at 60 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate samples for at least 30 minutes at 60 °C. If samples are not completely solubilised after 30 minutes, allow for a longer incubation (45 minutes to 1 hour).

steps.title: After incubation, transfer any samples in 1
steps.instruction: After incubation, transfer any samples in 1.5 mL BioMasher tubes to fresh 2 mL microcentrifuge tubes using a wide bore pipette – label these 2 mL microcentrifuge tubes for each sample. Samples that are already in 2 mL microcentrifuge tubes do not require transfer into new tubes.

steps.title: Transfer samples to the fume hood...
steps.instruction: Transfer samples to the fume hood for chloroform separation, then add 350 μL of chloroform:isoamyl (24:1) to each sample.

steps.title: Transfer samples to the HulaMixer™, rotating...
steps.instruction: Transfer samples to the HulaMixer™, rotating at 8 rpm for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Centrifuge samples at 10,000 g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge samples at 10,000 g for 2 minutes at room temperature. The samples will then have three distinct phases as seen below in Figure 1: an upper aqueous phase containing the gDNA, a milky interphase containing inhibitors and contaminants, and a lower organic phase containing denatured proteins. Centrifuged sample with three distinct phases.

steps.title: Return samples to the fume hood...
steps.instruction: Return samples to the fume hood in order to transfer the upper aqueous phase to a clean 2 mL microcentrifuge tube using a wide-bore pipette tip. Avoid transferring the milky interphase containing contaminants and inhibitors.

steps.title: Add 1 X volume of BL buffer to each sample
steps.instruction: Add 1 X volume of BL buffer to each sample. For example, if 400 μL aqueous upper phase was recovered in Step 10, add 400 μL of BL buffer.

steps.title: Add 10 μL RNase A to each sample
steps.instruction: Add 10 μL RNase A to each sample. Pipette-mix samples 15 times with a wide-bore pipette tip.

steps.title: Incubate samples for 10 minutes at 70 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate samples for 10 minutes at 70 °C.

steps.title: Label five 1 mL 96-well deep-well...
steps.instruction: Label five 1 mL 96-well deep-well KingFisher™ plates with the following labels, and fill all applicable wells of each plate with their corresponding reagents (see table below). Plate Name Reagent(s) required 1 Tip Plate 96-well tip comb (no reagents) 2 Sample Plate ~500 μL sample + ~500 μL SpeedBead solution 3 Ethanol Wash 1 Plate 1 mL freshly prepared 80% EtOH 4 Ethanol Wash 2 Plate 1 mL freshly prepared 80% EtOH 5 Elution Plate 400 μL buffer EB

steps.title: Remove samples from the heat block...
steps.instruction: Remove samples from the heat block and allow to cool to room temperature.

steps.title: Using a wide bore pipette tip,...
steps.instruction: Using a wide bore pipette tip, set the volume to 600 µL, transfer lysate from the now room temperature sample tubes into individual wells in the sample plate, taking care not to transfer large pieces of debris. Sample volume varies from 500–600 µL - it is important to note the volume as this will affect the maximum volume of SpeedBead solution that can be added.

steps.title: Add 400–500 μL Sera-Mag™ SpeedBead solution to each sample
steps.instruction: Add 400–500 μL Sera-Mag™ SpeedBead solution to each sample. The Sera-Mag™ SpeedBead solution should be vortexed before use to ensure that the beads are resuspended. Ensure that the total sample + SpeedBead solution volume does not exceed 1 mL e.g. for a 600 μL sample, only 400 μL of SpeedBead solution can be added.

steps.title: Select the required DNA extraction protocol...
steps.instruction: Select the required DNA extraction protocol in the protocol list on the KingFisher™ Apex (details below in KingFisher™ Apex DNA Extraction Protocol section/attached file within the Materials section) and select using the play button.

steps.title: Load the filled plates onto the...
steps.instruction: Load the filled plates onto the instrument following the instructions provided on screen.

steps.title: Once the final plate is loaded,...
steps.instruction: Once the final plate is loaded, the protocol will automatically begin; this takes approximately 90 minutes.

steps.title: Once the protocol has completed, follow...
steps.instruction: Once the protocol has completed, follow the on-screen instructions to remove plates from the instrument.

steps.title: Using a wide-bore pipette tip, carefully...
steps.instruction: Using a wide-bore pipette tip, carefully transfer the 400 μL EB buffer containing purified gDNA from the Elution plate into new 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes. If proceeding to the 0.45X SPRI, samples can remain in the Elution plate – this will then become the 'Sample plate' for the 0.45X SPRI.

steps.title: Set-up the KingFisher™ plates for the 0
steps.instruction: Set-up the KingFisher™ plates for the 0.45X SPRI as detailed below: Plate Plate Type Reagent(s) required 1 Tip Plate 1 mL Deep-well 96-well tip comb (no reagent) 2 Sample Plate 1 mL Deep-well 400 µL DNA + 180 µL AMPure PB beads 3 Ethanol Wash Plate 1 mL Deep-well 1 mL 80% EtOH (freshly made) 4 Elution Plate 200 µL standard 135 µL Buffer EB

steps.title: Select the required 0
steps.instruction: Select the required 0.45X SPRI protocol in the protocol list on the KingFisher™ Apex (details below in KingFisher™ Apex 0.45X SPRI Protocol section/attached file within the Materials section) and select using the play button.

steps.title: Load the filled plates onto the...
steps.instruction: Load the filled plates onto the instrument, following the instructions provided on screen.

steps.title: Once the final plate is loaded,...
steps.instruction: Once the final plate is loaded, the protocol will automatically begin; this will take approximately 45 minutes.

steps.title: Once the protocol has completed, follow...
steps.instruction: Once the protocol has completed, follow the on-screen instructions to remove plates from the instrument.

steps.title: Using a wide-bore pipette tip, transfer...
steps.instruction: Using a wide-bore pipette tip, transfer the 130 µL of eluate from the elution plate into new microcentrifuge tubes.

steps.title: Incubate the DNA at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Incubate the DNA at room temperature overnight and perform the required QC the following morning.

steps.title: Store the DNA at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Store the DNA at 4 °C.
